BITTE BITTE KAUFT EUCH DIESES SPIEL NICHT!!! Wenn ihr es geschenkt bekommt, GEBT ES ZURÜCK!!! Dieses Spiel ist seit einem 3/4 Jahr unverändert, die Bugs sind die selben wie an Tag eins, ja es macht kurz Spaß... ABER nach so vielen Fehlern die ganz eindeutig spielseitig passieren, treibt dieses Spiel einen zur Weißglut. Die Entwickler haben das Geld statt in Gameplay oder Bugfixing, in Fußballer Kooperationen gesteckt wobei man Skins mit ECHTGELD erwerben kann, die einem natürlich auch keinen Vorteil im Spiel bringen. Mehr ist seit Release NICHT passiert. Das wars. Ich verstehe warum dieses Spiel stirbt, bzw. schon seit Wochen Meter unter der Erde liegt. Es wird kein Wert auf die Nutzer gelegt, nur das Geld spielt eine Rolle und daran geht es kaputt. Es wird nur gemolken was das Zeug hält. Außerdem sind 25€ auch wirklich VIEL zu viel Geld für so ein minderwertiges und einseitiges Spiel, es gibt lediglich 1 einzigen Modus zu dritt viert oder fünft, ranked zu dritt oder fünft (warum nicht auch zu viert???) und es gibt noch LimitedTimeModes, die für sage und schreibe 5, JA 5 ganze Tage zu spielen sind. 

Bitte nehm dir meine Worte zu Herzen und überleg es dir genau, ich bin mir sicher 3,5 Döner von dem Geld würden sich mehr lohnen. 

LG an Sloclap ich hoffe ihr geht pleite ;)

EDIT: 10h weitergespielt und endlich gelöscht, mein Leben ist jetzt besser. Das Spiel sollte nur in Anwesenheit eines Psychiaters gespielt werden!!!

The game is sadly now in it's dying state, there are various problems such as goal keepers running out to the other side of the field with no punishment to throw the game, lenient punishments for toxic, afk and sabotaging players that are either ignored or slapped with a 10 minute ban at most, the other problem is consistent nerfs to animations, that reduce the response of your character in the game leading to various frustrating moments with barely any reward, this also comes along with the least played role of "Goalkeeper" being nerfed every season which led to even worse experience in gameplay unless you are the highest rank possible. 

Due to the development of the game staggering at that area almost all of the players that originally played have left. There are many more problems with this game in cases where I played games in which I haven't received a single pass just because teamplay or CO-OP within the game is extensively rare unless you go out of your way to look for a LFG (Looking for Group) channel which are also now almost entirely empty. Once the developers choose to address this issue I am sure that the game will thrive but that depends if anything is actually done about this. 

Lastly the game is unique, the animation and all of the mechanics are a masterpiece but again they are being worsened as the game progresses through its development.

Rematch tem uma proposta muito interessante de colocar você no papel de um player totalmente customizado em partidas de futebol online 3v3, 4v4 e 5v5. Até o momento as mecânicas de movimentação são um pouco limitadas, já que algumas animações podem ser bem lentas, dificultando tanto o ataque quanto a defesa, além de algumas delas serem bugadas. O jogo é bem divertido de se jogar, já passei algumas horas e nesse quesito ele atende muito bem!! Mas a sua experiência pode ser ruim dependendo da partida que você joga. Em modos de ranqueada terá momentos em que seu colega de equipe vai simplesmente abandonar o gol e tentar driblar o time adversário inteiro, não vai te passar a bola e no final pode acabar entregando a partida, o que acaba sendo bem frustrante

Veredito: Não espere muito desse jogo, já que até o momento a empresa está adicionando animações aos poucos e também corrigindo alguns bugs. Eu aconselho esperar o jogo abaixar mais o preço ou sair de graça, não recomendo pagar o preço cheio nem mesmo pagar pelas versões mais caras...

Bu yorumu 600'ü aşkın bir süre oyunu oynadıktan sonra yazıyorum, oynamaya da devam edeceğim.

Oyun galaktik futbol çizgi serisinden veya yeni jenerasyonun daha yakından bilebileceğini BlueLock animesinden ilham alınmış veya istemeyerek de olsa o evrene benzetilmiş bir rekabetçi futbol oyunu.

Oyunda FIFA, FC yada PES gibi takımı kontrol etmiyorsunuz, yarattığınız ve özelleştirdiğiniz karakteriniz ile sahadaki diğer oyuncular tarafından kontrol edilen 10 futbolcudan biri de sizsiniz. Bu oyun tarzının diğer futbol oyunlarından farklı ve güzel olan tarafı şudur; kendinizi bir halısahanın ya da profesyonel bir stadyumun içerisinde maç yapan bir futbolcu gibi düşünebilir, bu hissiyatı yakalayabilirsiniz.

Hatanızı telafi edecek bir bot sistemi ya da yapay zeka tarafından defans yapan birileri, boşa kaçıp pas opsiyonu yaratabilecek bir bot ve hatta şutları tutabilecek PC tarafından yönetilen bir kaleci bile yok. Hepsi oyuncu hepsinin bir rolü ve üstlenmesi gereken sorumluluğu var. Tam da bu yüzden çok daha gerçek çok daha rekabetçi!

Daha önce benzerleri yapıldı bu türün, yerli yapım I Can Football, Freestyle Football veya Football Superstars gibi oyunları bilenler REMATCH'e tüm bunların "hah şimdi olmuş" diyebilir. Çünkü bu tarz oyunlarını bilen ve arzulayan bir oyuncu için gerçekten muazzam bir oyun REMATCH ve beklentileri büyük oranda karşılıyor.

Oyunun eksiği veya kötü yanı yok mu? Elbette var. Ama bu üzümde çöp aramaya benzer. Yılların FIFA'sında olduğu gibi bi oyunda da netcode kaynaklı buglar ya da anlamsızca uçup kaçmalar var. Ama betadan bu yana gelinen bu versiyonda artık çok az karşılaşıyor. Oyunun yapımcıları oyundaki her türlü denge için santim santim ayarlamaları hala yapıyor.

Oyuncu sayısındaki düşüşün sebebini merak edebilirsiniz; kendimce şuna yorarım; içerik eksik. Rekabetçi bir oyunda kesinlikle olması gereken hele ki bu bir futbol oyunu ise; takım kurma, takım forması oluşturma, stadyum özelleştirme, takım vs takım ligleri, sıralamalar bunlar yok. 

Bence en büyük eksiklik bunlar, bunlar olmadığı zaman insanlar bir şekilde boşa düşüyor. Ranked sistemi bile anlamını yitirmiş durumda.

Tahminimce ve oyuncular arasında dönen dedikodulara göre oyunun yapımcıları teknik tarafı büyük ölçüde hallettikten sonra oyunu ücretsiz yapabilir veya bir takım anlaşmalar ile ikinci bir hype dalgası yaratabilir. Çünkü hali hazırda REMATCH'in Adidas, PUMA gibi markalarla anlaşması var.

Yeni bir "hype" dalgası yaratmak çok zor olmayacaktır çünkü oyunun bir benzeri veya eşi yok. Tek oyuncu kontrollü, rekabetçi ve 3D bir futbol oyunu oynamak istiyorsanız tek adresiniz REMATCH.

Oyunu kesinlikle tavsiye ediyorum, yanınızda oyunu bilen birileri olduğu sürece ya da denk geldiği sürece oyundan alacağınız zevk çok üst seviyelerde.

Receta de pastel de papas tradicional
Ingredientes
2 kilos de papas
500 gramos de carne picada
1 cebolla grande
2 dientes de ajo
1/2 taza de caldo de carne
1 zanahoria
1 morrón rojo
1 cucharada de tomate concentrado
200 gramos de queso rallado
Aceite de oliva c/n
Sal y pimienta a gusto

Pelar las papas y cortarlas en pedazos grandes. Ponerlas a hervir en una olla con agua y sal hasta que estén bien cocidas.
Escurrir las papas. En la misma olla que se cocinaron, aplastarlas hasta obtener un puré. Añadir sal y pimienta al gusto.
Picar la cebolla, el ajo, el morrón y la zanahoria.
En una sartén grande calentar el aceite de oliva a fuego medio.
Añadir la cebolla y el ajo, saltear hasta que estén dorados. Agregar la carne picada y cocinar bien hasta que quede marrón. Añadir la zanahoria y el morrón. Continuar cocinando hasta que estén tiernos.
Añadir el tomate concentrado, el caldo de carne, la sal y la pimienta. Cocinar a fuego lento durante 15 minutos, revolviendo ocasionalmente, hasta que el líquido se reduzca.
Precalentar el horno a 180 °C.
Ponerle manteca o aceite a un molde rectangular para evitar que el pastel se pegue. Colocar una capa de puré de papas en el fondo del molde, extendiéndolo de manera uniforme.
Luego, intercalar una capa de relleno de carne con una capa de puré hasta que se terminen los ingredientes. Finalizar con una capa de puré de papas.
Agregar queso rallado en la capa superior. Llevar al horno durante aproximadamente 40 minutos, o hasta que la capa superior esté dorada.
Retirar del horno y dejar enfriar durante unos minutos para luego servirlo mientras esté caliente.

Ce jeu a tout pour me plaire, j'ai passé des centaines d'heure sur Rocket League et j'ai adoré découvrir ce jeu, mais la j'en peux plus. Trop de bug / lag, trop de situations injustes ou tu passes une seconde avec le ballon mais finalement le jeu décides que non. 
Certains joueurs savent abuser du jeu pour dribbler a l'infini en l'air, et quand tu sautes au lieu d'être récompensé, tu interceptes le lob mais c'est quand même le dribbleur qui récupère la balle, depuis la dernière maj le goal sert a faire de la figuration, une passoire. Quand j'essaye de dribbler je me prends des tacles glissé WIFI, les mecs qui contrôlent la balle en Rainbow ( R1 + croix ) Pareil contrôle en wifi a 3 mètre alors que tu es sur la balle ils la mettent en l'air.
Je joue a des jeux de combat, de tir compétitif, je suis en Ethernet j'ai aucun lag dans les autres jeux. Le souci de connexion ne viens pas de moi.
Y a bien des parties ou tout se passe bien, ou le jeu est normal, mais la majorité du temps c'est avec un décalage qui rend le jeu difficile à apprécier.

EDIT J'essaye encore d'aimer ce jeu mais impossible. C'est trop inconsistant. Je tacle un gars a la fin de son dribble ca fait rien, au debut ca fait rien, on me tire dessus a 1 metre la balle me traverse, Moi je me fait tacler au début de mon dribble, a la fin de mon dribble, en fait tout ce qu'on m'apprend en entrainement dans le jeu ne s'applique pas sur le terrain en ligne. Je parle meme pas du saut (triangle) totalement inutile car le sombrero passe quand meme au dessus ou a coté ou a travers toi... alors que c'est censé etre l'outil d'interception. En fait c'est triste mais quand on joue contre des nuls on gagne, meme si le jeu lagge, contre une equipe de meme niveau le lag est très frustrant car cela nous coute des points et parfois la victoire, mais contre une equipe plus forte ou on doit tout donner, le moindre raté en défense c'est quasiment but en fait. donc si mon adversaire me traverse, ou que mon tacle n'arrete pas la balle ca sert a rien de jouer. 
J'ai joué a des jeux cross platform, je joue a des jeux de combats, ou il y a enormément a apprendre, j'ai fini les darks souls les elden ring sans guide donc niveau patience je me pose quand meme la. Mais ce jeu mes potes se demande si c'est vraiment moi qui joue tellement je suis en colère en vocal.
C'est simple : le joueur fait le bon choix, il est récompensé. Il fait le mauvais choix il est pénalisé. La je fais le bon choix je suis pénalisé. Donc je suis en colère.

Et encore pire avec la maj du plongeon du gardien qui le rends vraiment inutile. Il aurait du etre nerf dans les interactions avec un joueur adverse pour renforcer l'attaquant 1v1 contre le goal, ou le goal pouvait juste plonger sans réfléchir pour gagner la majorité des intéractions  pas le rendre inutile meme contre un tir en dehors de la zone de réparation qui arrive dans un coin de la cage.

El juego es sí es genial, buenas mecánicas fáciles de aprender y difíciles de dominar. 

Pero no puedo recomendarlo. Lo jugué cuando salió y lo deje por el mismo motivo que se que después de una semana de juego tras casi un año, lo volveré a dejar.

La gente.

No hay reglas, por lo que no hay fuera de fuego. Esto tiene un punto negativo, SIEMPRE, y repito. SIEMPRE como mínimo de tu equipo de 5 personas 1 estara arriba, generalmente ni siquiera sabrá jugar bien, sencillamente solo se sabe el botón de pedir el pase pero ninguno más, mucho menos la combinación de botones que te lleva a bajar a defender.

Pero eso es el mínimo, en mi experiencia, siempre han sido dos.

También tenéis al que se pone a driblar y cuando es contra el aire, todo perfecto. Pero es un 5 vs 5 y eventualmente llega cualquiera y se la quita, y como ni dios defiende acabó con el portero dos o tres contra nosotros. 

Y OJO, no criticó a la gente por fallar o ser mala. Eso lo hacemos todos, no somos perfectos. Puedes fallar 30 ocasiones y que el contrario te meta la primera, es cosa del fútbol. Habló de GILIPOLLECES. 

Quedan menos de 3000 jugadores, yo volveré a dejar el juego y este se que eventualmente morirá por sus jugadores. Y sí, cada uno juega como quiera, pero luego no te quejes de que nadie quiere jugar contigo, por eso los desarrolladores, que ya no estoy seguro de podrían estar a tiempo, deberían meter mano en la forma de jugarlo.

En fin, las mecanicas increibles, pero es solo ONLINE y pronto estará muerto

The biggest problem I have with this game in Rematch is the desync—it’s been really frustrating. Players with lower ping have a clear advantage since their gameplay is smooth, while those of us with higher ping deal with teleporting, stuttering, and inconsistent movement. It just doesn’t feel fair.

What’s even more disappointing is that this has been an issue for about a year now and still hasn’t been properly fixed. Instead, it feels like the developers are focusing more on adding new items to the store rather than improving the actual gameplay.

The ranked system also feels really frustrating, especially when playing solo. You can end up matched with Bronze players in a Platinum bracket, which creates a huge imbalance and makes matches feel unfair. It takes away from the competitiveness and makes it harder to take ranked seriously.

I do enjoy the game, which makes this even more frustrating. I just hope the developers start prioritizing stability and fair play instead of cosmetic updates.

This is my favourite type of game, a teamwork oriented experience with room for you to showcase individual talent and brilliance to outmanoeuvre the opposition to create advantages. Some people say that its a football game, some say its closer to basketball and others will compare it more to a fighting game. Each interpretation is valid as there are aspects of all 3 in this. Firstly and most obviously this game is visually and thematically a 5v5 football game which I would argue is closer to footballs less attractive cousin futsal. Secondly, the court style arena effective set plays and movements more solely resemble basketball tactics and finally learning to read each individual animation and training yourself to have the speed an intensity to react to that will make you more effective in those one on ones and that is very reminiscent of a fighting game. 

the community for this game I have found is a mixed bag but what I can say with certainty is that there is a community for every type of player in this game. In my server KRO we focus on building chemistry between individuals leaning into our pre-existing strengths and giving each person the opportunity to do what makes the game fun for them even if it isn't always the most effective decision. We also like to breakdown replays and discuss tactics and try new potential set ups and organise scrimmages with other servers so that we can test ourselves. there are however servers for the egoists, servers for the casual fun time gamers and also servers for many different languages. 

The entry level for this game often referred to in games as a skill floor I would say is higher than most games. the initial investment into effectively learning to do even the most basic of actions can take hours. passing, dribbling, shooting, tackling, body blocking, diving, sliding and jumping all require you to understand the momentum you are carry while going into them, the power at which you will do it and the effect the angle of your approach will have in the games physics. But as soon as you have all of these skills down to a basic level then the skill ceiling isn't much further. What sets apart a bad player and a decent one is mechanical efficacy and what sets a decent player and an amazing player are patience, the ability to read your opposition and your understanding of not just your own tactics but breaking down your opponents so that you can make them less effective.

If you are still here and you haven't gone off and downloaded it already I am guessing you are wanting to know what the issues with the game is  and unfortunately there are a few. Firstly the servers the game is hosted on have a tendency to randomly kick players, lag and reposition you on the pitch. During a 0-0 high intensity game when you are 3 or 4 minutes into overtime and that next goal wins or loses you the game having an opponent dc due to a weak server and to win because you had a numbers advantage feels really bad. Especially when the opposition is giving you a great game. However as you can imagine losing under those circumstance is far worse. if the games were not typically 5 minutes to 10 minutes long I would probably be more upset about it. Another big issue is greedy players. people who either solo Q or join the official discord looking for a team and spend games not passing, co-ordinating or working with the team can leave a sour taste in your mouth especially when they spot you making a mistake and spam the "good job" button. It can and often does very quickly devolve into a flaming contest which also ruins the games for your opponents. This can and does result in players mid match scoring against their own keeper out of spite and yet again ruining the experience for everyone. 

I think this game would benefit from more sponsorships and partnerships but for that to happen its E-sports community will need to pick up and for that prize money tournaments need to become more spectacular and enticing. They also need stablished E-sports professionals to come into the space and bring pre-existing audiences to watch them play. I would suggest a tournament being held with big streamers being invited to help with this but I think the thing which is most egregious is the rules for tournament play. 

The devs of this game I think have done a great job in most ways but the way they handle tournament rules is very backwards in my opinion because they have rules which penalise and punish you for making good tactical decisions. Such as; making it mandatory for you to press which means that even if you are winning and the opposition have the ball you cannot make the decision to park the bus and use a deep defensive line to give the opposition fewer gaps to exploit. I believe tis damages the experience of a tournament because allowing teams to do this will put greater emphasis on singular moments of brilliance to break the press. Also it is not exploitable because if you are drawing or losing that low block will not be effective at helping you win. Another rule is that you cannot pass back to your goalkeeper more than twice during a singular possession. the reason for this is that they believe it will be an exploitable way to prevent the opposition from getting the ball by using the open unmarked player as a passing option however just not allowing this doesn't make for an interesting watch I would love to see a team who is behind against the low block make the risky decision to push the goalkeeper up into teh role of a pivot and allow your other players to go deep, rotating and repositioning until the free player is in a position to exploit that and equalise. This would make the game far more thrilling to watch. Also teams that use cheesy bad faith tactics can make for great villains when watching a tournament and watching someone eventually devour that cheese will make them hero's they should make tournament play a reflection of a premade 5's scrim where both teams are trying to impress their identity upon the game that ay we can see a natural evolution of tactics and become engrossed in the experience of that. it gives commentators more to discuss and will allow for great context to even the most simple unattractive looking goals. That is what makes real football so thrilling. 

Finally and my last complaint with this game is that there is not steam achievement for being invited to a game by john rematch XD

Thank you for taking the time to read this, I hope it was helpful have a lovely day.

Rematch é aquele tipo de experiência que foca totalmente na pureza da competição e na agilidade das partidas, entregando um loop de jogabilidade tão fluido que se torna quase impossível fechar o jogo após apenas um round. O grande mérito do título reside no equilíbrio impecável entre a acessibilidade imediata e uma profundidade técnica que só se revela conforme você mergulha em seus sistemas.

A mecânica é visceral e direta, priorizando o tempo de reação e a leitura precisa dos movimentos adversários, o que gera uma sensação constante de evolução. Visualmente, o jogo opta por uma estética limpa e funcional que mantém o foco no que realmente importa, enquanto a trilha sonora dita um ritmo energético que amplifica a urgência de cada embate.

É um título que respeita profundamente o tempo do jogador, sendo perfeito para sessões rápidas, mas que possui camadas estratégicas em suas builds e loadouts para prender quem busca dominar cada detalhe. No fim das contas, ele entrega exatamente o que promete: partidas viciantes, performance técnica impecável e aquele desejo constante de apertar o botão de revanche imediata.

It's a game that constantly tries to remind you that it doesn't feel like a finished game. There are flaws on some core aspects of the game that hasn't been fixed yet: You intercept the ball from the enemy yet the game doesn't recognize it, you save balls yet it doesn't recognize it.
Apart from that, it constantly punishes you for playing it with random people. If someone disconnects from the game 5 seconds in, you're ♥♥♥♥♥♥. Constantly battling with afks, trolls and dcs in a way that the game feels like it doesn't try to help you out on the slightless, and it happens so much that it's just impossible to have a good time with the game (And I say it as someone who played LoL for years, this feels even worse than that). 
Also, there is no way to really communicate simple commands with your teammates, there are tons of commands missing. You should be able to say different things like: "look for openings", "run", "go center", "play slow", etc etc.
It's an amazing game that should have a big early access on the front, but it doesn't, and until it feels like a complete game, the review is going to be negative.
Also, there is this bug since the launch of the game that if your controller loses connection, the game doesn't recognize it mid game. If you don't launch the game with the controller on, you won't be able to use it. The fact that if for whatever reason your control turns off for 2 seconds or loses signal, you are stuck playing the whole duration of the ongoing game without it and have to restart the game to be able to use it is diabolical, should have been a fix week 1 or 2, and instead its about to reach a year from official launch.
Amazing early access, fix the game.

reviews.review:
I really like the concept of this game and how unique it is. I also like how each game is 5 minutes similar to RL. However, im not gonna use this review to talk about the good things. I'd rather talk about things that matter.

1) When adding updates and pushing new content, thats great but what about useful updates? i dont care about many addidas shirts or shoes or socks you add in this game. I care about functional updates. Let me give you some examples.
                          i) Viewing other people's stats or their profile generally, match history, win rate, all of those
                          ii) Fixing matchmaking, as a level 155, why am i playing with someone who doesn't know how to sprint?
                         iii) I can see the development team putting in effort to fix bugs but i see more effort in adding addidas socks in this game. Bug fixes happen every season or couple months or whatever and that includes patching exploits. Personally, thats not desirable. Also why do i still hear my team mates mic even when i deafen. I have to do unmute, mute, undeafen, deafen combo just to fix it. Also my mic unmutes by itself every now and then.
                       iv) Leaver's penalty isnt really that punishing. So what if he cant play for 2 minutes? or 5 ? or 10? the player might already be planning to leave the game soon, so might as well ALT+F4.
                       v) When a goalkeeper leaves the game or the post, why dont we get a notification that theres no gk
                           
TLDR; I'd love for the devs to focus more on functional updates, more in depth stats, bug and exploit fixes than content updates. Check the subreddit and you can get an idea of how the players feel.

Rematch presenta una propuesta interesante dentro de su género, con mecánicas que buscan ofrecer una experiencia dinámica y competitiva. Sin embargo, en su estado actual, el juego evidencia problemas técnicos que afectan de forma directa la experiencia del usuario, especialmente en regiones como Latinoamérica.

Uno de los principales inconvenientes radica en la infraestructura de servidores. La latencia es inconsistente, con picos de lag que interrumpen partidas y afectan la precisión de las acciones. Este problema no parece aislado, sino recurrente, lo que sugiere una capacidad insuficiente o una distribución inadecuada de los servidores para cubrir la demanda global. Para los jugadores en Latinoamérica, esto se traduce en desventajas claras frente a usuarios de otras regiones.

En cuanto a las mecánicas del juego, se identifican varios bugs que impactan la jugabilidad. Entre ellos:

Acciones que no se registran correctamente (inputs perdidos o retrasados).
Interacciones inconsistentes con el entorno o con otros jugadores.
Fallos en la sincronización durante momentos críticos de la partida.

Estos errores no solo afectan la fluidez, sino que también comprometen la competitividad, generando frustración en la comunidad.

Dado el potencial del juego, es fundamental que el equipo de desarrollo priorice:

Optimización y expansión de servidores, especialmente en regiones como Latinoamérica.
Corrección de bugs en mecánicas clave, garantizando consistencia en la experiencia.
Mejora en la estabilidad general del sistema, reduciendo desconexiones y errores de sincronización.

Latinoamérica no es un mercado menor; es una parte activa y creciente del nicho de jugadores. Ignorar sus necesidades técnicas limita el crecimiento del juego y afecta su adopción a largo plazo.

En resumen, Rematch tiene una base prometedora, pero requiere mejoras urgentes en su infraestructura y estabilidad para alcanzar el nivel competitivo que propone.

Rematch is a fun game, especially with friends, but DESPERATELY needs more players. There are 2,245 players in-game as of me writing this review. This is already a tiny playerbase before you factor in the many server regions (10 at launch) across the world, and two core gamemodes of Quick Matches and Ranked. There is 3v3, 4v4 and 5v5 for quick matches, and then 3v3 or 5v5 for ranked matches. 

In its current state, despite the fun gameplay, I sadly cannot reccomend this game. In my opinion, the devs need to make the game free-to-play. They already have the monetisation model, (excluding the game price obviously) with a battlepass and cosmetic store. The upcoming World Cup 2026 would be the perfect time to go f2p imo. Coincide an f2p relaunch with a crazy World Cup event?

There are also still lots of small bugs that need ironing out. For example, yesterday my friend and I both witnessed a goalkeeper pick up the ball with their hands, then throw it to their feet. This was near the HALFWAY line... 

Some other changes I would like to see is a text chat option in game, with the option to disable/hide it of course. Also, an option to avoid teammates like in Overwatch (and probs other games). 

I really can't understand why they haven't already made the game f2p already tbh since its dire/grim state is rather obvious, plus you'd think that the store revenue would greatly increase with an influx of new players. I'm also speculating that people would be more open to spending money on cosmetics in a f2p game to support the devs. 

I will edit my review if neccesary.

TLDR: Rematch is currently a rough gem with potential to shine with a f2p relaunch and the ironing out of minor bugs. In it's current state, I cannot reccomend this game despite the very fun gameplay.

10/10. I would love to see the teleportation training as DLC.

Ayant passé 26 heures sur la bêta et déjà 4 heures sur la version finale, je peux confirmer que REMATCH est une excellente surprise. C'est un jeu original, avec des graphismes vraiment soignés qui font plaisir à voir.

Mon expérience :

En seulement 4 heures, j'ai déjà débloqué plus de la moitié des succès. La prise en main est intuitive, surtout si on a les bases de la bêta.

Gameplay : J'aime absolument tout dans ce jeu. Que ce soit le rôle, la stratégie ou l'action, le plaisir est là.

Ne vous laissez pas décourager par les chiffres actuels (environ 1 600 joueurs contre 92 000 à la bêta). C'est dommage pour un jeu de cette qualité, MAIS le matchmaking fonctionne à merveille. Je trouve des matchs en 5v5 (classés ou non) en quelques secondes, et au maximum en 1 minute.


C'est un jeu frais qui change de ce qu'on voit d'habitude. Si vous cherchez de l'originalité et de la fluidité, foncez. Le jeu mérite une communauté plus large, alors n'hésitez pas à rejoindre l'aventure !"

Ya platinado, casi a las 60 horas lo logre, les vengo a dejar mi opinion. Empezando por la compra, consegui la key en nuuvem me costo como 10 dolares, estaba en oferta, compre la version basica, no esperaba mucho del juego pero unos amigos me dijeron que lo comprara para jugar con ellos, cosa que no ha sucedido tal cual.
Primero dire que lo quise juguar con teclado y raton y la verdad fue un quebradero de cabeza, no me adapte rapido y la sufri bastante al principio, porque los primerops partidos rapidos, aveces te topas con gente top y la verdad fue una mala adaptacion y lenta. Tengo un control de PS5 un dualsense original. y preferi intentarlo con el control, y lo logre me acostumbre y dire que si tienen control lo usen es mas facil y sencillo dominar asi el juego en PC.

Ya sorteada la adaptacion, el juego requiere su tiempo para dominar, no es un juego que en 3 partidos la vas a romper, no, si esperas eso este no es tu juego, el juego esta diseñado para jugar en equipo, si no sabes jugar en equipo vas a sufrirla muchisimo, pero muchisimo, y si al principio tambien la vas a sufrir, porque muchas veces si te dan la pelota y falla un par de veces el gol, es altamente probable que ya no te la pasen, ahi es donde entiendes como funciona el juego, el cual es bajar a defender y subir cuando se tiene la pelota y asi sucesivamente, los jugadores que solo esperan arriba para meter el gol poco a poco son apartados, la gente va buscando el juego en equipo y cuando se juega asi se gana y se prevalece sobre todo en clasificatorio.

Veo a mucha gente criticando el juego y pidiendo el free to play, pero dudo mucho que suceda, y mas viendo las actualizaciones que se vienen, si compran el juego si les doy estos consejos, primero jueguen con control, segundo si tienen amigos jueguen con ellos y tercera, dejen su comentario aqui hasta que lo platinen si aun no lo platinan la verdad es dificil que opinen positivo, porque creo yo 60 horas no es sufiiciente para aprender a jugar bien, yo apenas empiezo a dominar un poco, soy oro 1 y la verdad es muy complicado ser bueno, porque hay competencia, pero eso te va haciendo mejor.

En pocas palabras si le traen ganas al juego comprenlo en oferta y segundo denle su tiempo y veran como mejora, aun tiene sus fallas pero ha mejorado mucho, mucha gente se queja, porque no saben jugar en equipo, pero una vez aprendiendo lo vas a disfrutar muchisimo.

+ динамично

Минусы:
- много одиночек-рандомов-единоличников
- мяч проходит сквозь модельки
- система автоопределения направления мяча порой мешает, а не помогает
- мячик/персонажи телепортируются, дистанции отбора не понятны (проблема есть как с пингом 120, там и с 40)

Итог: не соревнование с противником, а борьба с игрой, которая не даёт нормального понимания происходящего. Сырая заготовка для хорошей игры. Что Абзольвер, что Футбол у разрабов - слабые проекты. Лучше бы дальше делали файтинги на подобии Сифу. Там кайф.

English: I played the closed beta of this game, and it was one of the best gaming experiences I’ve had in a long time. It was fun both playing solo and with a team. At the time, the controls still had some issues, but the game clearly showed huge potential.
That early expectation is exactly what shapes my current opinion. If I had to describe REMATCH in one word, it would be: disappointment.
The game is innovative in many ways. It brings a level of dynamism, fluidity, and playmaking possibilities that no other football game has delivered before. And it does this in a way that isn’t limited only to football fans — the gameplay itself is simply fun. That’s what makes it so good.
At this point you might be thinking: “so far this review sounds positive — why is it negative?” Besides some meta balance issues and what feels like a lack of support from the developers, the biggest problem was the monetization and launch strategy.
In Brazil, the game launched at around R$90, which is expensive for a live-service title. When a game already has an entry price, players naturally expect fewer microtransactions. I believe the company struggled to maintain a strong flow of appealing free and paid cosmetics, which likely affected its revenue. But the main issue was the entry barrier: paying nearly $20 for a new and uncertain game is a tough sell for many players.
When I look at the current player numbers, I honestly think this game deserved much more. It’s currently stabilized at around 5,000 players, which doesn’t reflect the quality of its original concept.
I still believe the game can be saved. A price reduction — or even making it free-to-play — would help a lot. On top of that, stronger investment in marketing, streamers, and YouTube promotion would make a big difference, along with better-quality cosmetics (both free and paid) and fixes to broken mechanics and meta issues (which are secondary for now).
I bought the early access version, and what I want most is for this game to become free-to-play so I can return with my friends and see an active and relevant community again.
This game is dying, but there’s still time to save it.
Don’t let REMATCH die.


Portugues: Joguei o beta fechado desse jogo e foi uma das melhores experiências que tive com videogames em muito tempo. Ele me trouxe diversão tanto jogando sozinho quanto em equipe. Na época, os controles ainda tinham alguns problemas, mas o jogo demonstrava um potencial enorme.
Essa expectativa que criei durante o beta é justamente o que motiva minha opinião atual. Se eu pudesse descrever REMATCH em uma palavra, seria: decepção.
O jogo é inovador em vários aspectos. Ele traz um estilo de dinamismo, fluidez e possibilidades de jogadas que nenhum outro jogo de futebol apresentou antes, e faz isso de uma forma divertida, que não se limita apenas a quem já gosta de futebol. A gameplay é gostosa e é por isso que ele é tão bom.
Nesse momento, você pode estar lendo essa análise e pensando: “até agora são só elogios, por que então a avaliação é negativa”. Além de alguns problemas com o meta e uma certa sensação de abandono por parte da empresa, o principal problema foi a estratégia de monetização e de lançamento.
O jogo foi lançado no Brasil por cerca de 90 reais, um preço alto para um live service. A partir do momento em que é um jogo pago, espera-se menos microtransações, já que o jogador já pagou pela licença. Acredito que a empresa teve dificuldades em manter um fluxo interessante de cosméticos gratuitos e pagos, o que provavelmente impactou a rentabilidade. Porém, o maior problema foi a barreira de entrada: pagar quase 100 reais por um jogo novo e incerto é complicado para muita gente.
Hoje vejo a quantidade de jogadores ativos e penso que esse jogo merecia muito mais. Ele está estabilizado em cerca de 5.000 jogadores, o que não condiz com a qualidade da ideia original.
Ainda acredito que existe salvação para o jogo. Uma redução no preço ou até torná-lo gratuito ajudaria muito. Além disso, seria importante investir pesado em marketing, streamers e YouTube, junto com a criação de cosméticos gratuitos e pagos de qualidade, além de ajustes no meta e correções de mecânicas quebradas (algo secundário no momento).
Comprei a versão de acesso antecipado e a coisa que eu mais queria é que esse jogo se tornasse gratuito, para que eu pudesse voltar a jogar com meus amigos e com uma comunidade ativa e relevante novamente.
Esse jogo está morrendo, mas ainda existe uma chance de salvação.
Não deixem o REMATCH morrer.

Во первых играть в нее без полной команды практически нет смысла. Потому что подбор игроков просто ужаснейший. В твою команду может закинуть как новичка который впервые зашел в игру так и человека который заучил в ней все механики(Но мне чаще всего попадаются такие в противоположной команде).И что самое прикольно даже в рейтинговых матчах такие встречаются, не понимаю как это происходит. Игра хорошая и надеюсь разработчики в какой то момент будут заниматься именно доработкой игры а не добавлением чего то нового

Joguei Rematch esperando algo inovador, mas a experiência acabou sendo bem decepcionante. O jogo tenta misturar futebol com elementos arcade exagerados, lembrando muito uma espécie de “Fortnite com bola”, mas essa combinação simplesmente não funciona tão bem quanto deveria.

A jogabilidade é confusa e pouco precisa. Em vez de transmitir a sensação de controle e estratégia típica de um jogo de futebol, tudo parece caótico e aleatório. Muitas vezes, você sente que o resultado das jogadas depende mais da sorte do que da habilidade.

Visualmente, o jogo até tenta ser estiloso, com cores vibrantes e efeitos chamativos, mas isso acaba contribuindo para a poluição visual. Em vários momentos, fica difícil até acompanhar o que está acontecendo na partida.

Outro ponto fraco é a falta de profundidade. Depois de algumas horas, o jogo se torna repetitivo e não oferece motivos suficientes para continuar jogando. Falta variedade, modos interessantes ou um sistema de progressão que realmente engaje.

Esse é o melhor, sim, o melhor jogo de futebol competitivo já feito ( e deixa o fifa no
chinelo). É claro que se trata de uma proposta diferente de um fifa da vida, são propostas de jogo diferentes, más quando falo de um jogo de futebol competitivo e justo, rematch é uma obra prima.
Aqui não tem aqueles sistemas de Overall pay to win, o passe do game é puramente cosmético.
No rematch, oque vai definir se você é um bom jogador ou ruim é unicamente sua habilidade e capacidade de ler o jogo, tomar boas decisões, resumindo é habilidade e noção de futebol.
 Você pode ser um cara muito
técnico, que saiba fazer passes e bons chutes, se você não souber se posicionar
para receber a bola ou ter visão de jogo para um passe oportuno para seu companheiro de time que vai sair na cara do gol ( e talvez perder), o seu desempenho não vai fluir.
Da mesma forma que não adianta você ser um baita "gênio tático " e nao saber fazer um simples passe ou lançamento para um companheiro bem posicionado, é provavel que você receba muitos ´´ MANDOU BEM!´´ até lá.
Considero rematch como a versão mais honesta de um jogo de futebol competivo, é instigante e com uma
curva de aprendizado justa. Desde o lançamento o game melhorou muito e, infelizmente até chegar isso a comunidade perdeu mais de 90% de sua base. Metas quebrados, pixeis manjados, nerfs em goleiro, demorou MUITO para a Noclap tomar uma atitude, más posso dizer que após jogar todas as temporadas, finalmente o jogo está equilibrado e em sua melhor versão.
É claro que o preço é um fator muito decisivo, se fosse hoje eu NUNCA gastaria 70 reais com esse jogo porque eu to duro rss, então realmente é o que acredito mais afastar novos players e o crescimento da base de jogadores.
Espero que fique gratuito logo para que mais pessoas conheçam essa OBRA PRIMA, se
divirtam e agreguem a nossa comunidade. Em um país que ama o futebol como o
Brasil, MERECEMOS um jogo além do caça-níquel Fifa.
Irmão se vc tem a grana e quer jogar um jogo de futebol divertido (principalmente com amigos) COMPRA, voce nao vai se arrepender. Se você ta sem grana espera ficar gratuito, acredito que não vá demorar :)

World Cup fever will soon be here, people with a passing interest in football will be enraptured with the advertising and emotion behind the sport and begin to look for a way to get closer to the sport in a digital way - the question is, should you buy REMATCH? 

This game has developed a loyal following despite the very severe flaws with it, so that goes someway to showing you how engaging and fun the core game play can be. 

However - should YOU buy this game?

To answer that I would first ask you to look at the game Absolver, an older sloclap release and you will begin to see some issues, a game that had unique mechanics and amazing movement that was abandoned by developers and player-base alike. The reviews mention that the core campaign takes 2-3 hours to complete and whilst there is a significant amount to do online, there are very few players to do any of that with and thus it becomes locked - so the game is dead. 

If you then look at the community posts on steam-forum for that Game you'll see that beyond the abandoning of the game and it's community there are very similar patterns - Absolver released with poor netcode and poor match-making which led to an exodus of players. 

Now all of that is fine, not every game can live on as a thriving multiplayer gem - however the developers still want to sell Absolver for 24.99 which doesn't make the company look very good given the value the game has left to offer right now. 

REMATCH released with a similar buzz, the Reddit community was full of hopeful players attempting to look past the dodgy net-code and just enjoy the game however the initial issue for skilled players was that the match-making system paired them with the lowest skilled players and this caused the skilled players to get frustrated and leave and so an increasingly loyal core group remained who just wanted to enjoy it.  

But then Sloclap began to change things in a hap-dash manner clearly unsure of what their direction was and every time they introduced things, new bugs or issues appeared - suddenly there was an update that introduced a meta where the ball was launched high into the box and the priority led to skilled strikers more or less scoring, then another update nerfed the dribbling, then another one introduced a pooped-your-pants crab walk - worse still is that sloclap appeared to be listening to the loudest parts of it's community which created a very myopic view of the wider player-bases feelings. 

And in-between all of these large issues were other game-breaking issues like sometimes not being able to touch the ball, frequent roll-backs causing your goal-keeping inputs to be undone, season pass broken and more. 

It had become clear that buyers were the testers for this game but the developers weren't showing that there was any clear plan to take the game to the next level.

Players got frustrated that despite the very serious issues in this addictive game, the design and marketing team were pumping out skins and cosmetics wanting more cash from people who had already purchased the game, whilst delivering an erratic inconsistent experience. 

Even more players left and eventually it became (and still is) hard to even find a game in some regions of the world (check the steam forums for this game to see real experiences). 

And then we come to the fundamental issue with this game: It is highly dependent upon the players you play with and you only need to look at recent reviews to see the type of player-base that is left. 

So Unless you have a large group of friends or some kind of group/organization looking for a fun team-play based game then for someone just looking to pick-this up I don't advise you buy it. 

And finally with the World Cup I imagine that Sloclap are going to look to push this game hard and at this point the optics are going to seem really positive because the only voices pushing it will be the Masochistic reddit users who respond to dev-updates in an embarrassingly servile tone and the devs who appear to be promising a lot - be careful, the patterns of this developer do not indicate a significant turn-around for this title. 

TLDR; I wouldn't recommend you buy this unless Sloclap make it free-to-play, I think you might have some fun initially but you also might struggle to find a game, the quality of the players on team when you do is likely to be erratic or toxic which will prevent you climbing the ranks and so you'll grow angry and then bored quickly. 

If you want a taste of collaborative football I'd recommend Fifa Pro-clubs in FC26, a mode where you play a position of your choosing in one of the 11 on the pitch and get a much better overall game as you can play a whole career offline in this way too and of course enjoy the other elements of a much bigger, polished game.

I have been playing this Game Since Beta. This game has huge potential and i still love playing it but in all my heart i just cannot give it a positive review. This game according to developers has been in work for 4 years while it looks and should have taken like 6 months maximum 1 year. 4 Years of development for an insane amount of bugs. The Beta was supposed to help that but on relase there were even more bugs. Every Patch they fixed some bugs but with every patch new bugs appeared. The list contains, Desync (a word that i have never used and never so often than in this game), The ball counting as score while being in the middle of the field, not being able to touch the ball at all, unable to leave queue even though entering the prompt and especially that the controller gets/counts as disconnected even though i just used it while the score was shot. etc. i probably forgot some on the way. This game also contains a lot of physic and Logic mistakes when it comes to players colliding with the ball, fouling. The last thing i want to mention which i think every football/Soccer game is struggling with is the goalie and his movement how he catches and so on. Movement and catch logic are not fully there but that has been sadly a unsolvable prompt for all soccer games. All in All its fun when it works but the constant bug issues just ruin the expereince and old bugs made their way back with new patches. After almost a year this just gets frustrating and ruins the fun of this Game.

I have absolutely loved this game since I started at the very end of Season 1. Played all of Season 2 and while the progression through the pass felt a little stagnant with it just being XP based with no challenges to speed it up, it was enough that by the end of the season I had almost every item and definitely every item I wanted and while I played a decent bit, I didn't feel like I had to play every day. However for season 3 which is only a little longer than 2 months they completely changed the progress system. With the XP system last season you got one token around every 2.5 matches and got a nice boost worth almost 3/4s of a token for you first win each day. Now you get 1 token every 5 games played. This is supposed to be balanced with the daily and weekly challenges which if you complete all weekly and your daily each day you get 14 tokens plus what you earned from just playing. Depending on you skill and how much time you can to afford to play this is also a big if. However even if you get all the challenges done they do not feel like they make up the difference at all since you need almost 200 tokens to get everything in this 9 or 10 week season which is a good bit more than was needed last season. I hope they remedy this issue before the end of the season because games like these with pretty much one thing going on need a sense of progress to feel good playing in my opinion and right now the progress just feels frustrating. I feel that these changes were made to try and inflate their player numbers by requiring more time from the players they have but I worry it will do the opposite and drive away any players not willing to make this their main game that they almost exclusively play.

Me atrevo a decir que este juego uno de los sueños húmedos de todo aquel que le gusten los juegos de futbol... o eso me esperaba.

La primera vez que descubrí Rematch sentí que estaba ante el juego que finalmente iba a destronar al FIFA y a los gordos mierderos de EA, algo que es tan sencillo como tomar buenas decisiones con el juego y viniendo del mismo estudio del tan aclamado Sifu pensé "Claro que no puede salir mal".

Probe la Beta y me encanto, podías hacer regates de varios tipos, muchos pases y hasta jugar de arquero si es que te gustaba. (No hubieron muchos cambios o añadidos a estas características por el momento)

Claro que cualquier bug o problema que me encontrara pensé que era normal ya que era una "Beta" y lo iban a solucionar.

Si bien hubo una que otra mejora no fue WOW, pero todavía era bastante disfrutable.

Lo que si que no es disfrutable son los gordos pelotudos que entran a trollear a las partidas y se piensan que están en Blue Lock que no dan 1 solo puto pase, en serio que creo que eso es lo peor del juego y lo malo es que no existe una sanción como tal. (que yo sepa)

Ósea pueden entrar a trollear en Ranked y yo no puedo hacer nada... seria bueno que los creadores probaran su propio juego.

También tenemos la opción de personalizar nuestro personaje a gusto, desde el genero hasta la ropa.

Algo muy lindo aunque hay ropa que normalmente no se usaría para jugar al futbol y medio que te saca de onda, porque terminas encontrando a gente con conjuntos de PUMA XD.

En resumen; es un juego muy bueno, con una que otra cosa mala pero es muy entretenido y mas si podes llegar a jugarlo con algún amigo. [b]8/10[/b]

Играл ещё с бета-теста. С того времени игра технически никак не изменилась, а в некоторых аспектах даже стала хуже. 

Всё ещё очень много проблем с рассинхронизацией мяча (к примеру, когда мяч просто остается на месте, хотя я пробивал его вперед). И проблема даже не в пинге. Проблема может встречаться даже на тренировке. Также встречаются слишком частые проблемы с привязкой к мячу, когда возможность взять мяч есть, но этого не происходит.

Игра вратаря - неблагодарная вещь.  В связи с технической проблемой игры, пропустить мяч на воротах проще простого, а мамины "эгоисты" сразу начнут спамить в чат.

Командной игрой в начале тут и не пахнет. Лишь иногда могут встретиться адекватные, командные игроки, с которыми возможно играть в пас.

Игра ни технически, ни геймплейно не развилась с момента бета-теста. Лишь стало больше кастомизации и доната (не удивительно). Цена на игру не оправдана. Хотя идея и начальная реализация была очень даже замечательной.

Играть бесплатно можно, покупать - нет.

The ranked system in this game is one of the worst I’ve seen in a competitive title. Matchmaking feels completely unbalanced and I’m constantly put against players way above my level with them being Masters/Elites while my teammates are noticeably weaker, making most matches feel unfair from the start.

Ranking up also doesn’t feel rewarding at all, since wins and losses don’t reflect actual performance. Team balancing is inconsistent, and games often feel one-sided.

Recent updates also made gameplay worse I feel. Movement feels sluggish, tackling is clunky and unreliable, and inputs don’t always feel the best responsive. On top of that all the server issues like lag and delay make things even more frustrating.

Casual mode is actually fun which i would recommend, but ranked clearly needs more time and major fixes. I wouldn’t recommend it right now for anyone looking for a very fair competitive experience.

Игра, к сожалению, с каждым обновлением становится только хуже. Вместо того чтобы удерживать онлайн и улучшать игровой процесс, разработчики Rematch выпускают патчи, которые лишь усложняют игру и ломают базовые механики.

После последнего обновления:

- Вратари были ослаблены — мячи не берутся даже при правильной позиции и тайминге.
- В защите при контактах возникают телепортации, откидывания и баги.
- Мяч может «магнититься» к ногам соперника без какого-либо взаимодействия.
- Часто мяч просто проходит сквозь модель персонажа.
- Хитбокс мяча работает некорректно — перехваты, особенно в прыжке, не засчитываются.

Разработчики обещали убрать спорные механики дриблинга и баги, но по факту они остались и даже стали заметнее.

Играю с самого начала и могу сказать — проект стал не сложнее, а хуже технически. Многие тиммейты уже ушли по этим причинам. Онлайн падает, и желание играть тоже.

Unfortunately, the game keeps getting worse with every update. Instead of focusing on retaining players and improving the overall experience, the developers of Rematch release patches that only make the game more frustrating and break core mechanics.

After the latest update:

- Goalkeepers have been nerfed — they fail to save shots even with correct positioning and timing.
- Defensive interactions are broken — collisions often result in teleporting, knockbacks, and random bugs.
- The ball can “magnetize” to opponents’ feet without any actual interaction.
- The ball frequently passes straight through player models.
- Ball hitbox is inconsistent — interceptions, especially while jumping, often don’t register.

The developers promised to fix controversial dribbling mechanics and related bugs, but in reality, they are still present and even more noticeable now.

I’ve been playing since launch, and I can confidently say the game hasn’t become more challenging — it has become technically worse. Many of my teammates have already quit because of these issues. The player base is declining, and so is the desire to keep playing.

Проблема Rematch в том, что разработчики двигаются в противоположном направлении от того, что нужно игре. Вместо исправления ошибок и удержания игроков, каждое обновление только усугубляет ситуацию.

Ключевые проблемы после последнего патча:

— Ослабленные вратари: даже при правильной реакции и позиции мячи не берутся.
— Некорректные столкновения: при борьбе за мяч появляются телепортации, откидывания и баги.
— «Магнитный» мяч: соперник может просто пробежать рядом — и мяч окажется у него.
— Прохождение мяча сквозь модель персонажа.
— Поломанные хитбоксы: очевидные перехваты, особенно в прыжке, не засчитываются.

Это не ощущается как «повышение сложности» — это ощущается как потеря контроля над игрой.

Дополнительно разочаровывает несоответствие обещаний: заявлялось, что спорные приёмы и баги дриблинга будут исправлены, но на практике они только усилились.

Я играю с релиза и могу подтвердить, что состояние игры ухудшается от обновления к обновлению. Многие игроки из моего окружения уже ушли по этим причинам. Онлайн падает, и при таком подходе это закономерно.

Ce jeu a des bonnes mécaniques de gameplay, sur ca rien a dire. par contre le jeu est sorti depuis presque un an et aucune nouveauté, toujours les mêmes modes 3v3 ou 5v5 et de temps en temps (tous les 6 mois) un mode très moyen. Il y a un réel manque d'ambition. Très décevant sur un tout autre point c'est les microtransactions qui sont devenu de plus en plus présente.  

Mais le plus gros problème du jeu et de très loin, c'est l'aspect communautaire. En classé les joueurs qui joueront avec vous seront souvent bien moins bon et malheureusement aucun moyen permet de savoir si on joue avec des gens de notre niveau ou non. De plus aucun système de signalement n'est inclus, ce qui fait que certaines personne s'amusent encore après près d'un an a lancer des parties sans jouer ou a jouer contre leurs propre équipe.

Un jeu qui avait un gros potentiel mais qui malheureusement finira sans doute par se détruire par son manque d'ambition.

Sincérement ce jeu a tout pour vous faire passer un bon moments entres amis ou même seul avec d'autres joueurs.
Mais malheureusement le jeu n'est pas du tout équilibré. Quand tu es platine et qu'on te mets dans ton équipe des joueurs argents... tout le plaisir part en éclat et il ne reste que de la frustration. Ca plus les joueurs toxiques qui se déconnectent en pleine partie classée et qui peuvent revenir jouer quelques minutes plus tard.... Vous créez un jeu sur le principe génial, pour en faire de la merde derriere. Dans n'importe quel métier que le votre vous seriez licenciés pour incompétence. Faites quelque chose SVP. Equilibrage en classé, et un systeme de signalement ou des plus lourdes sanction pour ceux qui se déconnectent en classé. Merci.

I love this game to death, but even though i recommend it, i advise you to enter this game with the idea of what it is, and the player base in it.

Most people do not play this as a team based game, rather a "Blue Lock" or "Main character syndrome" infestation is what populates it. Despite that, when you manage to find an active team that seeks to play it like a proper football game, that's where the game shines. Its a blend between real football adding some arcade elements to it, but not without its flaws. It's still riddled with clunky interactions with the ball to this day despite the devs trying to iron it out.

In my honest opinion, you're paying for a great football game that nothing else can simulate in the market, even invoking some nostalgia with the old fifa street series back in the day. But bear in mind, i believe the game is ironing itself out still, and when the foundation and gameplay are fully developed, it might open up and go free to play. Or die, but hopefully that's not the case.

All in all if you have some mates or a team to play with, definitely a good time.

Потенциал есть, но сообщество и разработчики делают всё, чтобы игра сдохла.

Играл в REMATCH с надеждой получить удовольствие от футбольного симулятора, но на деле получил только сеанс бесконечного разочарования. К сожалению, проект катится в бездну, и вот почему.

Начнем с комьюнити. Играть в команде - это сущее наказание. Половина вратарей в этой игре искренне считают себя нападающими: они бросают пустые ворота, бегут в атаку, закономерно теряют мяч, и мы получаем гол в свои ворота. Это не футбол, это детский сад. Про полевых игроков я вообще молчу: никто не знает, что такое кнопка паса. Вместо командной игры - бесконечные, бесполезные финты и удары в молоко из центра поля. Зачем играть в командный спорт, если ты эгоистичный индивидуалист с нулевым IQ?

Но главная беда не в игроках, а в самих разработчиках. Вместо того чтобы исправлять критические баги и полировать механики, они выкатывают обновления, которые только ломают то, что еще хоть как-то работало. Механику вратаря «убили» окончательно. Отборы и перехваты - это отдельная тема для слез: в 60% случаев ты идеально выходишь на мяч, но игра просто игнорирует твое действие, словно тебя там и нет.

Не рекомендую. 2/10 за потраченное время.

ゲーム自体のポテンシャルはどのゲームにも勝るものがあると思っています。
ただ、いかんせん人口が少なすぎて同じ人とマッチするのは当たり前でランクの概念は無いに等しいです。

味方が移動してくれなかったりパスを出してくれなかったり相手にフリーでボールが渡ってしまったりと瞬間的にイラっとすることは多々ありますが、それは相手が上手い場合や味方とのコミュニケーションエラーなど様々な要因があるので、イライラポイントではあるがそこを含めて楽しむのがRematchだと思っています。



人口を増やすために無料化など様々あると思いますが、まずは細かいチュートリアルを作ってほしいと心から願っています。

ボールに対してどのアプローチが正解なのか、はたまたバグなのか仕様なのかわからないことがよくあり、プレイしていてイライラすることが多くそういった様々な判定や仕様をゲーム内でチュートリアルとして説明する場所を設けてくれると、人口増加や新規プレイヤーの定着に繋がるのかなと個人的に思っています。

戦略的なチュートリアルやドリブルのチュートリアルもあればこれ以上言うことはないです。


現在のRematchは高水準なパーティー相手にどう対処するかというのがデフォルトなので、今のチュートリアルは何の意味も為していません。



最初にも言いましたがゲームとしては唯一無二でかなり面白いです。
トキシック（意図的なトロールやVCでの激昂）やリアル小中学生（VCの声判断）など多種多様な方とマッチしましたが、オンラインゲームとしては健全だと思うので「またこいつとか～」くらいで楽しむのが最善だと思います。
煽りのようなジェスチャーを連打する味方に対しては、寛大な心で耐え忍ぶか良いプレイをした際に「ナイス」と一回返してあげましょう。
きっと更生して良いRematchプレイヤーに生まれ変わってくれるはずです。
自分がムカついているときは味方も同様にムカついているかもしれないと思いましょう！

新規プレイヤーがより参入しこのゲームを続けてくれるよう願っています。

I believe this game has more potential than any other out there.
However, the player base is simply too small — it’s normal to match with the same players repeatedly, and the ranking system might as well not exist.

There are plenty of moments that can be frustrating — like when teammates don’t rotate, don’t pass, or leave opponents completely open. But these situations can happen for many reasons, such as the opponent simply being better or miscommunication within the team. While these are definitely frustrating, I think part of the enjoyment in Rematch comes from accepting and dealing with them.

To increase the player base, there are various options such as making the game free-to-play. However, more than anything, I sincerely hope they add more detailed tutorials.

There are many situations where it’s unclear what the correct approach to the ball is, or whether something is a bug or an intended mechanic. This often leads to frustration during gameplay. If the game included a place where these mechanics and various in-game decisions were clearly explained through tutorials, I believe it would help attract new players and improve retention.

It would be even better if there were strategic tutorials and dribbling tutorials as well — at that point, I’d have nothing more to ask for.

At the moment, Rematch essentially assumes you’re up against highly skilled, coordinated parties by default, so the current tutorial doesn’t really serve any meaningful purpose.

As I mentioned at the beginning, the game itself is truly unique and incredibly fun.
I’ve matched with all kinds of players — toxic ones (intentional trolling or raging over voice chat), as well as younger players judging by their voices — but overall, I think it’s fairly healthy for an online game. It’s best to just take it lightly, like “oh, it’s this guy again.”

If you have a teammate spamming taunt-like gestures, it’s probably best to either be patient and ignore it, or simply respond with a single “Nice” when they make a good play.
Who knows — maybe they’ll improve their attitude and grow into a better Rematch player.
And when you’re feeling frustrated, remember that your teammates might be just as frustrated as you are.

I really hope more new players will join and continue playing this game.


Wishing everyone a great Rematch experience!

J’ai longtemps hésité avant de savoir si je le recommandais. Bien sûr que j'ai envie de le recommander, parce que oui, j’ai passé de très bonnes soirées sur Rematch. A sa sortie, il y avait une vraie hype, du monde, de la découverte, du freestyle, et de vraies sensations de foot.

Mais voilà : le jeu n'est pas fini. Et depuis presque un an, il est toujours en "accès-anticipé" déguisé. 

Gardez à l'esprit que si vous achetez le jeu maintenant, vous devenez un bêta-testeur, amené à redécouvrir le jeu chaque saison, tant il y a des changements de gameplay. 

Ce qui me gêne surtout, c’est l’impression que chaque grosse mise à jour peut changer profondément l’équilibre du jeu. On peut aimer l’idée d’un jeu qui évolue, mais ici, l’équilibrage bouge beaucoup (beaucoup trop) et pas toujours dans un sens qui rend l’expérience plus agréable (tout en laissant actif les bugs, les problèmes de netcode et les "exploits" des saisons précédentes).

Le jeu a du potentiel, ça ne fait aucun doute. Mais force est de constater qu'il s'agit d’un excellent concept et non pas d'un jeu abouti.

En attendant une vraie version du jeu, en Free-to-Play un jour peut être ?

The game is not without it's flaws—it's a little too barebones still amongst other things like matchmaking gripes—but when it all comes together on the field it's downright electric. Orchestrating an organic and effective offense tickles my brain in a way that I can only compare to clutching in Counterstrike or comboing in SSBM. Praise I do not give lightly with 1.2k hours in CSGO and probably north of 10k hours in Melee. It's got skill expression all over and linking up with a good team is incredibly satisfying.

If you're getting into it just be warned that there is a very real learning curve and you will almost certainly spend your early hours learning a lot of different lessons the hard way. This game doesn't have the population size to keep you fully segregated to a new player pool and although it will do its best, you're gonna have to take your lumps as if you were picking up a new fighting game. Some basic soccer fundamentals will help a lot and teamwork is critical. I recommend Gold'ing all the challenges and spending a lot of time in freeplay mode to get comfortable with the systems.

If you enjoy games where you can tell how good a player is just by watching them for a few seconds, and can tell what kind of player they are by watching them for a few minutes, you'll find that depth to chew on here. Even after 300 hours and hitting max rank in 3v3, I still constantly see a variety of skillsets and styles in my games. Everyone plays with their own bit of personal flair in some way or another and I think that's kinda beautiful.

I would like to preface this review by saying that I don't like soccer or football as everyone outside of my country's border calls it. I played when I was about 8 years old and my coach kept benching me because I was better than the other kids. So when I "scored enough" i had to sit out. That made me quit and hate the game eventually, I know it shouldn't have but hey.. I was 8. And that's when i took up gaming.

That being said, this game is the best competitive sports game I have ever played for a number of reasons.  I currently have 104.9 hours in game on steam and an extra 373.95 hours on Xbox Game pass before they hiked the price of game pass up. 

This game, either inherently(due to the nature of soccer where mostly everyone has a similar build) or by design, solves the problem that NBA 2K has. There are no character builds, no body weight or length. No sliders for me to decide what my player is or isn't good at. No different animation packages for me to go on youtube and look up which is the best competitively. Everyone has access to the same tools. So games are decided purely by cooperation and who uses the moveset better. You will see glaring gaps in skill early on. This game has a mercy system which allows for the game to end immediately if a team leads by 4 goals. It happens often at first but as you get better and climb in rank and elo, the games are a lot closer. Some overtimes lasting 10 minutes. 

Offense feels good, defense feels good and goalie mostly feels good. There are times where the dive animation makes no sense making you jump over the ball but that rarely happens.

I just love this game. It is the most fair and balanced competitive sports game I have ever seen.

Even the players arent really toxic but mind you I play with voice chat off.

REMATCH: A Deep Dive After 1,700+ Hours (Goalkeeper Perspective)
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

After spending more than 1,700 hours in REMATCH and competing at a high level in various tournaments, I can confidently say this is one of the most rewarding competitive football experiences available. Whether you are a casual player or looking to go pro, the depth here is unmatched.

The Art of Goalkeeping
As someone who specializes as a goalkeeper, I’ve found that this role is incredibly nuanced. It’s not just about quick reflexes; it’s about mastery over:

Positioning: Anticipating the striker’s move and closing down angles before the shot is even taken.

Decision Making: Knowing exactly when to sweep out or stay on the line. At a high competitive level, a split-second decision determines the outcome of a match.

Communication: From the back, you have the best view of the pitch. The game allows you to effectively organize your defense and call out plays.

Advice for New Players
If you are just starting your journey in REMATCH, here is my advice:

Be Patient: The learning curve can be steep. Don’t get discouraged by early losses; use them to study the game’s physics.

Find Your Role: While it's good to be versatile, focusing on one position (like Goalkeeping) will help you stand out in the tournament scene.

Engage with the Community: The competitive side of the game is where the real fun is. Join teams, participate in trials, and learn from veteran players.

Final Verdict
REMATCH rewards dedication. My 1,700 hours have been a journey of constant improvement and thrilling tournament moments. If you’re looking for a football game that respects your time and rewards your skill, this is it.

I remember playing a demo of a similar game for the PSOne back in the day, and ever since then I have always wanted to have a single-person-controlled 3rd person football game, which neither Fifa nor PES provided, iirc. Mind you, I have not played any other football games besides Fifa 98/2000. Additionally, I am not invested in IRL football besides national tournaments.

The bad:

- The game can be absolutely janky, has bugs and terrible lag at times where it turns winning a match into losing one in seconds. Sometimes you just don't take the ball when it clearly touches you.

- Forget playing with mates who live on the other side of the world. High ping will deffo not help you make friends. So rather play in your ping-appropriate area. 

- There is (as yet) no way of rewatching previously played games. Would be cool to show off my crazy plays during my breaks next to the office water dispenser ;) 

- TURN VOICE CHAT OFF - most people forget to turn off their voice chat and you hear their whole household in the background. It surprises me that most people play next to a washing machine with their mothers in the back screaming at them lol

The good:

- It's actually fun to play casually and you can be creative with your own way of playing without being a cringe sweatlord/meta slave that has to follow every step of a guide they watched on youtube/tiktok. 

- Goalkeeper controls are easy to get into after some practice. 

- Shooting and passing mechanics work. 

- A well-coordinated and understanding team can make the experience very enjoyable. 

- No chatbox, so you can focus on the game instead of being a clown.

- Playing a premade team with friends is the most enjoyable experience.

- You can actually join an ongoing game! 

- 5 minutes per match works well for people with jobs. Hop in, play some games, and hop off.

The ugly = the community:

- For soloQs, like in other games, it will get really difficult to play with pre-made squads that only pass between each other and won't involve you at all. They will get all the points from passing while you sit there being ignored. They will ask you to pass the ball, but won't do it themselves.

- Like in Dota/LoL, you have the non-reflecting griefers that spam "very nice" for every little inconvenience and bring nothing to the table. They ragequit the first seconds after the other team scores a goal and blame everyone else but themselves for losing the game.

- The "They're not him/her" players who fatally overestimate themselves doing tricks  and dribbling instead of passing the ball. They are not necessarily mean-spirited but the game is 100% not for them. They have 0 awareness nor self-reflection and either can't or refuse to defend. I have seen good players who have once or twice carried the game, but they mostly lose you the games. You will encounter them a lot.

To Sum up:
The game has so much potential and is a rough diamond. If you have a group of friends to play with, it’s an easy recommend. If you're going solo, just keep your expectations in check, turn off the voice chat, and prepare to play with some "not him/her"s.

This game had so much potential. I played both betas, purchased the pre sale elite version and regret it to this day. 

The devs just fluked the game hard on launch, it came out unstable and still is to this day, well, it doesn't crash anymore, but there are many things that haven't been solved since launch. 

There are some jersey textures that are actually low res and pixels flood into the details and look bad overall. I still get ABSURD net coding issues, tackles not registering, adversary tackles hitting after the ball is not even with me anymore and teleporting to their feet, 40ms btw. Talking about ping, this is the only game where I get 40ms, all other multiplayer competitive games I get < 20ms.

There still are stupid mechanic errors like the "Last Shot" when a team is leading by 2 or more, if you as much as tap the ball forward to keep pace, it counts as the last shot and ends the match, and sometimes even when you shoot, instantly after leaving your control zone, the game ends, if you hit the goal, it doesn't count.

Also, the player base is still bad at the game, main character syndrome, no teamwork, toxic and it's just a terrible experience. They will try to dribble all of the other team on their own, lose the ball and still blame you.

At its core, the game still shows glimpses of what it could have been, but in its current state, it's difficult to recommend, even at a discount.

Ich kann dieses Spiel absolut nicht empfehlen. Das größte Problem ist das extrem schlechte SBMM (Skill-Based Matchmaking). Die Matches fühlen sich komplett unausgeglichen an – entweder spielt man gegen deutlich stärkere Gegner oder bekommt völlig unkoordinierte Teams zugewiesen. Von Fairness kann hier wirklich keine Rede sein.

Noch frustrierender sind jedoch die Mitspieler. In gefühlt jedem zweiten Spiel hat man es mit toxischen Teammates zu tun. Viele beleidigen, griefen absichtlich oder verlassen einfach mitten im Match das Spiel. Das zerstört jeglichen Spielspaß und macht ein ernsthaftes Spielen praktisch unmöglich.

Auch das Gameplay selbst ist alles andere als gelungen. Das Torwart-System wirkt unausgereift und unzuverlässig, während das Dribbling sich clunky und wenig responsiv anfühlt. Statt Kontrolle hat man oft das Gefühl, gegen die eigene Steuerung zu kämpfen.

Was besonders enttäuschend ist: Die Entwickler hatten in der Beta-Phase mehr als genug Zeit, diese Probleme zu beheben. Doch scheinbar hat sich absolut nichts verbessert. Viele der grundlegenden Schwächen sind immer noch vorhanden, was den Eindruck vermittelt, dass Feedback kaum berücksichtigt wurde.

Insgesamt ist Rematch in seinem aktuellen Zustand einfach nicht empfehlenswert. Wer ein faires, spaßiges und ausgereiftes Spielerlebnis sucht, sollte sich definitiv nach Alternativen umsehen.

I really wish this game had a bigger player base, because at its core, it’s a genuinely fun experience with a lot of potential.

Rematch does a great job rewarding good team play, and when you actually get a group that works together, it feels amazing. Pulling off coordinated plays and seeing everything click is easily the best part of the game.

That said, playing with randoms can be hit or miss. Most players don’t really lean into teamwork, which takes away from what makes the game shine.

It’s still fun, and I keep coming back to it, but I can’t help feeling like it never quite reached the audience it deserved. Maybe going free-to-play could give it the boost it needs.

Overall, a solid soccer game with great moments

I’ve never really been into football games, but Rematch actually surprised me. The character design is great and feels very inclusive. I love being able to choose my own gear, colors, and tattoos to really make the player my own. The sound effects are on point, and the way the maps change as you play keeps the vibes fresh.

As a beginner, there are a lot of mechanics to learn, and the "ball priority" can be tricky at first. I also ran into some bugs near the goal and found the ranking system a bit messy, especially when teammates quit mid-match and leave you at a disadvantage.

Despite the technical hiccups, it's a really positive experience. If you’re looking for a stylish and fun sports game, it’s definitely worth a try!
