[h1]FOREWORD[/h1]
This review will purely focus on the Performance and "fun" factor on the more casual side.
For something more competitive, please check out the other reviews. They will most likely elaborate better than i could in that regard. 
While i do host a competitive league and tournaments, my knowledge on the actual competitive mechanics and balancing of the game is limited. 
Here are some I've seen/know about from competitive players in the Community:
- https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198176675070/recommended/2138720/
(Will add more if i find more)

Side-note:
I welcome healthy discussions. If you critique my points, expect a reply. I value valid opinions but will correct baseless claims. 

Let’s get started.
[hr][/hr]
[h1]GRAPHICS AND PERFORMANCE[/h1]
Performance is an important part in any competitive title, sadly it falls short.
One of the problems is the use of Unreal Engine 5. 
Powerful, but not really that great for a competitive game in which high visibility and performance are important.
Just look at the performance disaster that is Oblivian Remastered.
[h2]The Negatives:[/h2]
[h3]No good non-temporal Antialiasing solutions:[/h3]
Anti-aliasing aka. "The stuff that makes edges less pixelated" is extremely important for players on systems that are not that great. Sadly, the options are not great.
We have FXAA and a selection of Temporal solutions.
DLSS/DLAA require specific hardware but people that don't have any AI Hardware Acceleration (1660 Ti for example) cannot even use them.
As a result, you have a Hardware-Dependent AA solution that also introduces smearing too.
We have TSR and TAA, which are known to cause a lot of smearing, which in a game where split second decisions matter, can make you go from "ok, the ball is going this way" to "what? i was not even able to see the ball going that way and i went the wrong way".
The frustration comes with the smear that is out of your control. 
This problem is lessened by FXAA, but not fully eliminated. FSR shares the same issue with smearing.
Solutions like proper SMAA or MSAA, are going to provide way more clarity while a lot of times also looking better.
So in my situation i have the following options:
[b]TAA or TSR[/b]
Image is blurry as soon as i move.
[b]FXAA[/b] 
Image is still blurry but less-so. Still enough to make me interpret shots from afar wrongly
[b]DLSS or DLAA[/b] 
I already am GPU bound and see no performance gain from DLSS or DLAA on any profile. Looks worse (due to AI upscaling compared to native rendering) and doesn't perform better. Also: Smearing.
[b]FSR[/b]
Is less good than DLSS or DLAA and smears quite a lot too
[b]No Anti-Aliasing[/b] 
Lets it look blocky like crazy.
Higher resolutions fix the "no AA" method i'm mentioning, but the next points elaborate why that, for me, is impossible.

There is no real good solution to use for visual clarity and good performance for me.
[h3]The Graphics don't justify the performance:[/h3]
A competitive game must balance visuals and performance, but here, it fails.
My system (specs below) barely hits 80 FPS on lowest settings. 
This isn't just "whining". Titles like CS2 or Valorant offer better visibility without being this taxing. 
High-end hardware is no excuse for poor optimization. 
While UE5 is heavy by default, Valorant's UE5 transition proves that high performance is possible with proper work.

[h3]The Hardware usage:[/h3]
My system (below) is lower mid-range for 2026 i'd say. Still, the game's visuals don't justify pinning my GPU at 100% on the lowest settings at 1080p. 
The VRAM is overutilized, causing mid-game stutters as assets are constantly reloaded. 
For a game that looks like this, such extreme hardware demand on minimum settings is unjustifiable.

[h2]The Positives:[/h2]
[h3]The Artstyle:[/h3]
Artstyle is a very subjective topic, but me? I like it.
This Artstyle sets up perfectly for all kinds of collaborations. Either with Real-Life football Pros and Legends or anything animated (There is no way that this game won't have a BlueLock collab at some point.)
It's pleasant on the eyes, makes it easily identifiable what happens (when the Anti-Aliasing methods don't impact it) and is done well enough to immerse you in the world of REMATCH.

[h3]Summary: Graphics and Performance[/h3]
The game runs "okay," but not well enough for a global competitive title. While regions like NA or EU might have the hardware, the high demand excludes players in other parts of the world who can't reach 60 FPS. The great artstyle and immersive effects don't justify this level of resource consumption. It looks fantastic, but the performance cost is simply too high for what is being delivered.
[hr][/hr]
[h1]IS IT FUN?[/h1]
♥♥♥♥ me... It is.
The game doesn't sugar-coat anything. You are the player, You are in control. No action is pre-calculated by the game. Only when you can interact with the ball. It's in your hands what happens.
When everything clicks you get tense matches and really rewarding gameplay when winning and extreme frustration when losing.
When it goes wrong you have trolling team-mates or the biggest egos there is. 
It's amazing how expressive you can be with gameplay while having a limited pre-configured selection of comms like "Good Job" or "Sorry".
And that also follows to the moves executed in-game. 
Simple dribbles, Passes that range from small lobs to very high shot passes, Momentum based movement, Non-momentum based movement through the defensive stance at the cost of speed. Freely shooting with self-decided curves in any X and Y axis.

From my casual eyes, it all clicks together in a way that can just be addicting. 
When playing with friends it's a ton of fun. It's fun for the same reasons as Rocket League. You're in control. And that results in really funny moments that can be remembered forever.
When it feels rewarding, it's REALLY rewarding. When it feels un-rewarding, it's REALLY un-rewarding. 
The balance in terms of fun has been done extremely well.

The Matchmaking for ranked does leave a lot to be desired and is one of the main reasons why I don't play.
I'm kind of... anti-social. I'm a guy that enjoys playing games solo if my friends don't want to play. While it does limit me in terms of who i play with, it should not limit my fun.
Getting Matched with Elite (highest rank) players as a Platinum is not something that makes the experience fun. 
While dwindling player counts might be the reason, it's still way too frustrating.
You can only watch as the Elite uses the most advanced and newest mechanics present in the game with no way to counter it. It frustrates a lot.

It's a lot of fun but the ranked issues restricts me from wanting to actually play the game.
I would've loved to put more hours into it and while a lot of the reasons why i don't are my own fault, it's not the first game i will turn to when i'm bored.

TL;DR
- Fix the performance.
- Add more competitive oriented Graphics options to improve clarity.
- Improve Ranked fairness when possible.

I want to see it succeed and i wish nothing but the best to Sloclap. But you don't judge potential in a review, you judge the finished product.
I can recommend the game.
But the mixed reviews show that many don't see it that way, which you also can't invalidate either.

Je recommande le jeu uniquement pour être à même de mettre un commentaire potentiellement visible par les développeurs absolument pas qualifié de Rematch. 

Le jeu à un potentiel de fou, il est amusant et il a tout pour faire un bon jeu SAUF des développeurs soucieux de le rendre meilleur.
Donc en gros, après avoir saigné la BETA comme pas possible, joué au jeu dès la sortie et qui continue à jouer encore aujourd'hui l'état du jeu est juste RI DI CULE. 
Pour celles et ceux qui souhaiteraient plus de détails, en 1 ans : une béta + deux saisons voici les changements : 
- de nouveaux cosmétiques

Donc en gros on est entrain de me dire que nous avons : un jeu qui a des lags serveurs en permanence, des bugs visuels, des bugs d'interaction, qui fait lag même le plus performant des pc depuis la BETA n'a toujours pas été réglé deux saisons plus tard ? oui oui c'est bien ça.

On a un jeu qui n'a pas changé d'un poil depuis 1 ans. Ce qui pourrait arrivé avec un jeu qui n'aurait pas de soucis, sauf que c'est bien loin d'être le cas.

J'ai rarement vu un jeu avec un tel potentiel qui peut vraiment être exceptionnel, être gâché à ce point. Je ne comprends pas ce que font les développeurs pour être honnête. Aucun changement n'est fait excepté pour les cosmétiques. 

Et je suis de loin le seul à penser cela, je me demande sérieusement si les développeurs en on quelque chose à faire de notre opinion et de l'expérience que nous avons sur le jeu.

Voila, sachez que si vous achetez le jeu vous allez aimer au début, c'est certain le jeu est vraiment bien. Mais soyez certain avant de l'acheter que vous allez rencontrer de nombreux bugs.

Rematch – Goalkeeper Gameplay Feedback

Controller stick sensitivity (Defensive Mode)
When playing with a controller, a small sideways stick movement makes the goalkeeper move slowly, which is fine. The problem appears when jumping for the ball.
The sensitivity threshold for jumping is different from movement, so the goalkeeper often jumps straight up instead of diving sideways. This is especially noticeable during quick reactions to shots or when changing direction.

Goalkeeper vs dribbling attackers
Goalkeepers currently have very limited options against dribbling players:

attacker animations feel faster

attackers sometimes snap/teleport to the ball

it is very difficult to take the ball when standing close

goalkeeper slide tackle is one of the slowest animations in the game

attackers can change direction faster than the goalkeeper dash

attackers have multiple offensive options in every situation (shoot in different directions, pass, or use the backboard), while the goalkeeper has very limited defensive tools to respond

This makes close-range defending very difficult.

Unsaveable shots
Even with perfect positioning and reaction time, some shots — especially high ground shots or blade shots — appear to be completely unsaveable for the goalkeeper. This makes certain goals feel unavoidable regardless of player skill.

Camera sensitivity inconsistency
Camera movement speed on controller is different in Free Look and Defensive Mode.
This makes orientation and precision harder. There should be an option to use the same sensitivity in both modes.

Visibility issues
Visibility becomes a big problem during crowded situations in the box:

player nicknames have white backgrounds

the ball is also white, making it hard to track during shots

large UI elements like Overtime or Last Defense cover the screen during critical moments

These elements reduce visibility exactly when players need it the most.
